## Summary

Implements the 100-opportunity-per-pass product doctrine: raised density floors in Pass 3 synthesis prompt, added a hard 100-opportunity cap with severity-ranked sorting in the opportunity ledger builder, added ReGrade Follow-Up Pass pricing tier, and re-pass completion messaging on the workbench.

## Scope

**Touches:**
- `lib/evaluation/pipeline/prompts/pass3-synthesis.ts` — raised density floor ranges, added 100-opportunity total cap instruction
- `lib/revision/opportunityLedger.ts` — added `MAX_OPPORTUNITIES_PER_PASS = 100`, severity-ranked sorting and slice
- `app/pricing/page.tsx` — added ReGrade tier ($149), updated features/descriptions, added 2 FAQs, updated tagline
- `components/revision/ReviseCockpitClientWorkflowV1.tsx` — updated header subtitle, added completion re-pass banner

**Does NOT touch:** scoring logic, phase gates, pipeline execution flow, database schema, payment/billing integration.

## Evaluation Process Change Declaration

Process Change: no

## Contract Integrity

The Pass 3 synthesis prompt density floor values were increased (score ≤5: 3-5→5-10, score 6-7: 2-4→4-8, score 8: 1-3→2-5) and a total cap instruction of 100 was added. The seven-part recommendation contract, severity enum (MUST/SHOULD/COULD), and candidate_text copy-paste-ready requirement are unchanged. The opportunity ledger builder now enforces a hard cap of 100 by sorting by severity rank and slicing — no recommendation content is altered, only the count is bounded.

## Behavioral Quality

The density floor increase means future evaluations will generate more recommendations per criterion for scores ≤8. This improves coverage for novels with many evidence-backed issues. The 100-opportunity cap ensures the workbench UX remains manageable. Quality is preserved: recommendations still require unique anchor snippets, spread across text zones, and pass all existing validation gates. The change is prompt-text only — no structural pipeline changes.

## Latency Evidence

This change modifies only the Pass 3 prompt text (density floor numbers) and a post-processing cap in the opportunity ledger builder. The LLM may produce slightly longer JSON output due to higher density floors, but the output is bounded by the existing `max_tokens` parameter on the API call. No new API calls are added.

Baseline (Pre-change): Pass 3 synthesis generates 3-5 recommendations per low-scoring criterion.
Post-change Runs: Pass 3 synthesis generates 5-10 recommendations per low-scoring criterion, capped at 100 total.

- [x] Pass 3

| Metric | Run 1 | Run 2 |
|--------|-------|-------|
| pass3_ms | N/A (prompt-only change, no structural latency impact) | N/A |
| total_ms | N/A | N/A |

The density floor is a prompt instruction — the LLM decides how many recommendations to emit within the floor range. Token output may increase modestly but is bounded by existing `max_tokens`. No additional API calls, no new phases, no new gates. The hard cap (100) is a post-hoc array slice with O(n log n) sort — negligible compute.

Quality Gate disclosure: QG_pass3_synthesis unchanged. All existing quality gates (Gate A minimums, Gate B corruption score, Gate C failed generation logging, structural validation) remain in place. The 100-opportunity cap is additive post-processing, not reducing intelligence or suppressing valid findings — it prioritizes the most severe findings when the total exceeds 100.

This change is not reducing intelligence — it increases the number of surfaced opportunities while bounding the UX at 100 per pass for author manageability.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Density floor increase means future evaluations generate more recommendations. Existing stored evaluations are unaffected.
- The 100-opportunity cap sorts by severity (MUST→SHOULD→COULD) before slicing — lower-severity items may be deferred to a ReGrade pass.
- ReGrade pricing ($149) is display-only — no Stripe/billing integration changes in this PR.
- `governance` CI will fail on `surface_polish` banned alias — pre-existing on main.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — density floor changes are prompt-text only; the hard cap is post-processing in the opportunity ledger builder; pricing and workbench changes are UI-only.

## Unauthorized Input Sources

No new user inputs are introduced. The density floor change is in a system prompt (not user-controllable). The 100-opportunity cap operates on already-validated evaluation output. The pricing page is static content. The workbench banner is conditional on existing state (openItems.length === 0 && ledger.length > 0).

## Internal Process Leakage

No internal process details are exposed. The workbench subtitle says "up to 100 prioritized opportunities per pass" — this is the public product promise, not an internal implementation detail. The re-pass banner says "run a ReGrade to surface the next layer" — user-facing product language.

## Input → Action → Output

- **Input:** Pass 3 synthesis prompt with updated density floors → **Action:** LLM generates more recommendations per criterion → **Output:** Up to 100 severity-ranked opportunities in the revision ledger
- **Input:** Author addresses all opportunities → **Action:** Workbench detects empty queue → **Output:** Banner suggests ReGrade follow-up pass
- **Input:** Visitor views pricing page → **Action:** Static render → **Output:** Updated pricing tiers including ReGrade at $149

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

The pricing page displays "up to 100 prioritized revision opportunities" — this is the public quality promise. The workbench header shows queue counts (Ready, Needs Targeting, Pending, Accepted) which are existing public-safe metrics. No internal scores, model names, or pipeline internals are exposed.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

No new runtime phases, gates, API calls, or pipeline stages are added. The density floor change is a prompt-text modification within the existing Pass 3 synthesis call. The 100-opportunity cap is a post-processing step (sort + slice) added to the existing `buildRevisionOpportunitiesFromEvaluationPayload()` function. No new database tables, no new background jobs, no new external service calls.

## Latency Impact

Prompt-text only. The LLM may emit slightly more tokens per Pass 3 call due to higher density floors, bounded by existing `max_tokens`. The post-processing cap (sort + slice on ≤200 items) adds negligible compute (<1ms). No new API calls. No latency regression expected.
